v.CNV: 以下是摩西在约旦河东的旷野，疏弗对面的亚拉巴，就是在巴兰和陀弗、拉班、哈洗录、底撒哈之间，向以色列人所说的话。
v.ESV: These are the words that Moses spoke to all Israel beyond the Jordan in the wilderness, in the Arabah opposite Suph, between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Dizahab.
v.GAE: 이는 모세가 요단 저쪽 숩 맞은편의 아라바 광야 곧 바란과 도벨과 라반과 하세롯과 디사합 사이에서 이스라엘 무리에게 선포한 말씀이니라
v.KJV: These be the words which Moses spake unto all Israel on this side Jordan in the wilderness, in the plain over against the Red sea, between Paran, and Tophel, and Laban, and Hazeroth, and Dizahab.
v.NASB: These are the words which Moses spoke to all Israel across the Jordan in the wilderness, in the Arabah opposite Suph, between Paran and Tophel and Laban and Hazeroth and Dizahab.
v.NIV: These are the words Moses spoke to all Israel in the wilderness east of the Jordan —that is, in the Arabah —opposite Suph, between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth and Dizahab.
v.NRSV: These are the words that Moses spoke to all Israel beyond the Jordan — in the wilderness, on the plain opposite Suph, between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Di-zahab.
v.SAENEW: 이것은 모세가 요단 강 동쪽 광야에서 모든 이스라엘 사람에게 선포한 말씀이다. 그 때에 그들은 숩 가까이에 있는 요단 계곡에 있었다. 한쪽에는 바란 마을이 있고, 다른 한쪽에는 도벨과 라반과 하세롯과 디사합의 여러 마을이 있는데, 바로 그 사이에 이스라엘 사람들이 있었다.
v.WLB: 이것은 모세가 요단 강 동쪽의 아라바 광야, 곧 바란, 도벨, 라반, 하세롯, 디사합 사이에서 모든 이스라엘 백성들에게 말한 것입니다.

v.CNV: 从何烈山经过西珥山的路，到达加低斯．巴尼亚，共有十一天的路程。
v.ESV: It is eleven days' journey from Horeb by the way of Mount Seir to Kadesh-barnea.
v.GAE: 호렙 산에서 세일 산을 지나 가데스 바네아까지 열 하룻길이었더라
v.KJV: ( There are eleven days’ journey from Horeb by the way of mount Seir unto Kadesh-barnea.)
v.NASB: It is eleven days' journey from Horeb by the way of Mount Seir to Kadesh-barnea.
v.NIV: (It takes eleven days to go from Horeb to Kadesh Barnea by the Mount Seir road.)
v.NRSV: (By the way of Mount Seir it takes eleven days to reach Kadesh-barnea from Horeb.)
v.SAENEW: (호렙에서 세일 산을 지나 가데스바네아까지는 열하루 길이다.)
v.WLB: 호렙 산에서 세일 산을 거쳐 가데스 바네아로 가는 데는 11일이 걸립니다.

v.CNV: 出埃及以后第四十年十一月一日，摩西照着耶和华吩咐他一切关于以色列人的话，都告诉了他们。
v.ESV: In the fortieth year, on the first day of the eleventh month, Moses spoke to the people of Israel according to all that the Lord had given him in commandment to them,
v.GAE: 마흔째 해 열한째 달 그 달 첫째 날에 모세가 이스라엘 자손에게 여호와께서 그들을 위하여 자기에게 주신 명령을 다 알렸으니
v.KJV: And it came to pass in the fortieth year, in the eleventh month, on the first day of the month, that Moses spake unto the children of Israel, according unto all that the LORD had given him in commandment unto them;
v.NASB: In the fortieth year, on the first day of the eleventh month, Moses spoke to the children of Israel, according to all that the LORD had commanded him to give to them,
v.NIV: In the fortieth year, on the first day of the eleventh month, Moses proclaimed to the Israelites all that the Lord had commanded him concerning them.
v.NRSV: In the fortieth year, on the first day of the eleventh month, Moses spoke to the Israelites just as the LORD had commanded him to speak to them.
v.SAENEW: 이집트에서 나온 지 사십 년째가 되는 해의 열한째 달 초하루에, 모세는 주님께서 이스라엘 자손에게 말하라고 명하신 모든 것을 그들에게 말하였다.
v.WLB: 40년째 되는 해 열한째 달 1일에 모세가 여호와께서 이스라엘 백성들을 위해 말씀하신 모든 것을 선포했습니다.

v.CNV: 当时，他已经击败了住在希实本的亚摩利人的王西宏，和住在亚斯他录与以得来的巴珊王噩。
v.ESV: after he had defeated Sihon the king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon, and Og the king of Bashan, who lived in Ashtaroth and in Edrei.
v.GAE: 그 때는 모세가 헤스본에 거주하는 아모리 왕 시혼을 쳐죽이고 에드레이에서 아스다롯에 거주하는 바산 왕 옥을 쳐죽인 후라
v.KJV: After he had slain Sihon the king of the Amorites, which dwelt in Heshbon, and Og the king of Bashan, which dwelt at Astaroth in Edrei:
v.NASB: after he had defeated Sihon the king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon, and Og the king of Bashan, who lived in Ashtaroth and Edrei.
v.NIV: This was after he had defeated Sihon king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, and at Edrei had defeated Og king of Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth.
v.NRSV: This was after he had defeated King Sihon of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, and King Og of Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth and in Edrei.
v.SAENEW: 이 때는 모세가 헤스본에 사는 아모리 왕 시혼을 치고, 아스다롯과 에드레이에 사는 바산 왕 옥을 무찌른 다음이었다.
v.WLB: 이때는 헤스본에서 다스리고 있던 아모리 사람들의 왕 시혼을 물리치고 아스다롯에서 다스리고 있던 바산 왕 옥을 에드레이에서 물리친 후였습니다.

v.CNV: 摩西在约旦河东的摩押地，开始讲解这律法，说：
v.ESV: Beyond the Jordan, in the land of Moab, Moses undertook to explain this law, saying,
v.GAE: 모세가 요단 저쪽 모압 땅에서 이 율법을 설명하기 시작하였더라 일렀으되
v.KJV: On this side Jordan, in the land of Moab, began Moses to declare this law, saying,
v.NASB: Across the Jordan in the land of Moab, Moses undertook to expound this law, saying,
v.NIV: East of the Jordan in the territory of Moab, Moses began to expound this law, saying:
v.NRSV: Beyond the Jordan in the land of Moab, Moses undertook to expound this law as follows:
v.SAENEW: 모세는 요단 강 동쪽 모압 땅에서 이 율법을 설명하기 시작하였다.
v.WLB: 요단 강 동쪽 모압의 영토에서 모세가 이 율법을 상세히 설명하며 말했습니다.

v.CNV: “耶和华我们的　神，在何烈山告诉我们：‘你们在这山上住够了；
v.ESV: “The Lord our God said to us in Horeb, ‘You have stayed long enough at this mountain.
v.GAE: 우리 하나님 여호와께서 호렙 산에서 우리에게 말씀하여 이르시기를 너희가 이 산에 거주한 지 오래니
v.KJV: The LORD our God spake unto us in Horeb, saying, Ye have dwelt long enough in this mount:
v.NASB: 'The LORD our God spoke to us at Horeb, saying, 'You have stayed long enough at this mountain.
v.NIV: The Lord our God said to us at Horeb, “You have stayed long enough at this mountain.
v.NRSV: The LORD our God spoke to us at Horeb, saying, “You have stayed long enough at this mountain.
v.SAENEW: "우리가 호렙 산에 있을 때에, 주 우리의 하나님이 우리에게 다음과 같이 말씀하셨습니다.""너희는 이 산에서 오랫동안 머물렀으니,
v.WLB: “우리 하나님 여호와께서 호렙 산에서 우리에게 말씀하셨다. ‘너희는 이 산에서 오랫동안 머물러 있었다.

v.CNV: 现在你们要转回，起程到亚摩利人的山地去，到那些住在亚拉巴、山地、低地、南地、沿海一带、迦南人的地、黎巴嫩，直到大河，就是幼发拉底河一带的地方去。
v.ESV: Turn and take your journey, and go to the hill country of the Amorites and to all their neighbors in the Arabah, in the hill country and in the lowland and in the Negeb and by the seacoast, the land of the Canaanites, and Lebanon, as far as the great river, the river Euphrates.
v.GAE: 방향을 돌려 행진하여 아모리 족속의 산지로 가고 그 근방 곳곳으로 가고 아라바와 산지와 평지와 네겝과 해변과 가나안 족속의 땅과 레바논과 큰 강 유브라데까지 가라
v.KJV: Turn you, and take your journey, and go to the mount of the Amorites, and unto all the places nigh thereunto, in the plain, in the hills, and in the vale, and in the south, and by the sea side, to the land of the Canaanites, and unto Lebanon, unto the great river, the river Euphrates.
v.NASB: 'Turn and set your journey, and go to the hill country of the Amorites, and to all their neighbors in the Arabah, in the hill country and in the lowland and in the Negev and by the seacoast, the land of the Canaanites, and Lebanon, as far as the great river, the river Euphrates.
v.NIV: Break camp and advance into the hill country of the Amorites; go to all the neighboring peoples in the Arabah, in the mountains, in the western foothills, in the Negev and along the coast, to the land of the Canaanites and to Lebanon, as far as the great river, the Euphrates.
v.NRSV: Resume your journey, and go into the hill country of the Amorites as well as into the neighboring regions — the Arabah, the hill country, the Shephelah, the Negeb, and the seacoast — the land of the Canaanites and the Lebanon, as far as the great river, the river Euphrates.
v.SAENEW: 이제 방향을 바꾸어 나아가, 아모리 사람의 산지로 가거라. 그 인근 모든 지역, 곧 아라바와 산지와 평지와 남부 지역과 해변으로 가거라. 또 가나안 사람의 땅과 레바논과 저 멀리 있는 큰 강, 유프라테스 강까지 가거라.
v.WLB: 진영을 철수하고 아모리 사람들의 산지로 들어가라. 아라바와 산지들과 서쪽 산 아래 평지와 그 남쪽과 해변을 따라 사는 모든 이웃 민족들과 가나안 사람들의 땅과 레바논으로 저 큰 강 유프라테스까지 가라.

v.CNV: 看哪，我把这地摆在你面前，你们要进去，占领这地为业，就是耶和华向你们列祖亚伯拉罕、以撒、雅各起誓应许给他们和他们的后裔的地。’
v.ESV: See, I have set the land before you. Go in and take possession of the land that the Lord swore to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give to them and to their offspring after them.’
v.GAE: 내가 너희의 조상 아브라함과 이삭과 야곱에게 맹세하여 그들과 그들의 후손에게 주리라 한 땅이 너희 앞에 있으니 들어가서 그 땅을 차지할지니라
v.KJV: Behold, I have set the land before you: go in and possess the land which the LORD sware unto your fathers, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, to give unto them and to their seed after them.
v.NASB: 'See, I have placed the land before you; go in and possess the land which the LORD swore to give to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to them and their descendants after them.'
v.NIV: See, I have given you this land . Go in and take possession of the land the Lord swore he would give to your fathers—to Abraham, Isaac and Jacob—and to their descendants after them.”
v.NRSV: See, I have set the land before you; go in and take possession of the land that I swore to your ancestors, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give to them and to their descendants after them.”
v.SAENEW: 내가 너희 앞에 보여 주는 이 땅은, 나 주가 너희에게 주겠다고, 너희 조상 아브라함과 이삭과 야곱에게 맹세한 땅이니, 너희는 그리로 가서 그 땅을 차지하여라."
v.WLB: 보라. 내가 너희에게 이 땅을 주었다. 들어가서 여호와께서 맹세하며 너희 조상들, 곧 아브라함과 이삭과 야곱에게, 또 그들 뒤에 계속될 후손들에게 주겠다고 하신 그 땅을 차지하라.’”

v.CNV: “那时，我曾经对你们说：‘你们的重担，我独自一人无法担当。
v.ESV: “At that time I said to you, ‘I am not able to bear you by myself.
v.GAE: 그 때에 내가 너희에게 말하여 이르기를 나는 홀로 너희의 짐을 질 수 없도다
v.KJV: And I spake unto you at that time, saying, I am not able to bear you myself alone:
v.NASB: 'I spoke to you at that time, saying, 'I am not able to bear the burden of you alone.
v.NIV: At that time I said to you, “You are too heavy a burden for me to carry alone.
v.NRSV: At that time I said to you, “I am unable by myself to bear you.
v.SAENEW: "그 때에 내가 당신들에게 말하였습니다. '당신들을 지도할 책임을 나 혼자서 질 수 없습니다.
v.WLB: “그때 내가 너희에게 말했다. ‘나 혼자서는 너희를 감당할 수 없다.

v.CNV: 耶和华你们的　神使你们多起来；你们看，你们今日像天上的星那样多。
v.ESV: The Lord your God has multiplied you, and behold, you are today as numerous as the stars of heaven.
v.GAE: 너희의 하나님 여호와께서 너희를 번성하게 하셨으므로 너희가 오늘날 하늘의 별 같이 많거니와
v.KJV: The LORD your God hath multiplied you, and, behold, ye are this day as the stars of heaven for multitude.
v.NASB: 'The LORD your God has multiplied you, and behold, you are this day like the stars of heaven in number.
v.NIV: The Lord your God has increased your numbers so that today you are as numerous as the stars in the sky.
v.NRSV: The LORD your God has multiplied you, so that today you are as numerous as the stars of heaven.
v.SAENEW: 주 당신들의 하나님이 당신들을 불어나게 하셔서, 오늘날 당신들이 하늘의 별처럼 많아졌기 때문입니다.
v.WLB: 너희 하나님 여호와께서 너희 수를 늘리셔서 너희는 오늘 하늘의 별들만큼 그 수가 많아졌다.

v.CNV: 愿耶和华你们列祖的　神，使你们比现在更多千倍，又照着他应许你们的话，赐福给你们。
v.ESV: May the Lord , the God of your fathers, make you a thousand times as many as you are and bless you, as he has promised you!
v.GAE: 너희 조상의 하나님 여호와께서 너희를 현재보다 천 배나 많게 하시며 너희에게 허락하신 것과 같이 너희에게 복 주시기를 원하노라
v.KJV: (The LORD God of your fathers make you a thousand times so many more as ye are, and bless you, as he hath promised you!)
v.NASB: 'May the LORD, the God of your fathers, increase you a thousand-fold more than you are and bless you, just as He has promised you!
v.NIV: May the Lord , the God of your ancestors, increase you a thousand times and bless you as he has promised!
v.NRSV: May the LORD, the God of your ancestors, increase you a thousand times more and bless you, as he has promised you!
v.SAENEW: 주 당신들의 조상의 하나님이 당신들을 천 배나 더 많아지게 하시고, 약속하신 대로 당신들에게 복을 주실 것입니다.
v.WLB: 너희 조상들의 하나님 여호와께서 너희를 1,000배나 늘려 주시고 그분이 약속하신 복을 주시기를 빈다!

v.CNV: 我独自一人怎能担得起你们的重担、责任和你们争讼的事呢？
v.ESV: How can I bear by myself the weight and burden of you and your strife?
v.GAE: 그런즉 나 홀로 어찌 능히 너희의 괴로운 일과 너희의 힘겨운 일과 너희의 다투는 일을 담당할 수 있으랴
v.KJV: How can I myself alone bear your cumbrance, and your burden, and your strife?
v.NASB: 'How can I alone bear the load and burden of you and your strife?
v.NIV: But how can I bear your problems and your burdens and your disputes all by myself?
v.NRSV: But how can I bear the heavy burden of your disputes all by myself?
v.SAENEW: 그러니 나 혼자서 어떻게 당신들의 문제와 당신들의 무거운 짐과 당신들의 시비를 다 감당할 수 있겠습니까?
v.WLB: 그러나 내가 어떻게 너희의 괴로움과 너희의 짐들과 너희가 다투는 것을 혼자 다 감당할 수 있겠느냐?

v.CNV: 你们要按着支派选出一些有智慧、有见识、有经验的人，我就立他们作你们的首领。’
v.ESV: Choose for your tribes wise, understanding, and experienced men, and I will appoint them as your heads.’
v.GAE: 너희의 각 지파에서 지혜와 지식이 있는 인정 받는 자들을 택하라 내가 그들을 세워 너희 수령을 삼으리라 한즉
v.KJV: Take you wise men, and understanding, and known among your tribes, and I will make them rulers over you.
v.NASB: 'Choose wise and discerning and experienced men from your tribes, and I will appoint them as your heads.'
v.NIV: Choose some wise, understanding and respected men from each of your tribes, and I will set them over you.”
v.NRSV: Choose for each of your tribes individuals who are wise, discerning, and reputable to be your leaders.”
v.SAENEW: 당신들은 각 지파에서 지혜가 있고 분별력이 있고 경험이 많은 사람들을 뽑으십시오. 그러면 내가 그들을 당신들의 지도자로 세우겠습니다.'
v.WLB: 현명하고 통찰력 있고 존경할 만한 사람들을 너희 각 지파에서 뽑으라. 그러면 내가 그들로 너희를 다스리게 할 것이다.’

v.CNV: 你们回答我，说：‘你吩咐我们作的事很好。’
v.ESV: And you answered me, ‘The thing that you have spoken is good for us to do.’
v.GAE: 너희가 내게 대답하여 이르기를 당신의 말씀대로 하는 것이 좋다 하기에
v.KJV: And ye answered me, and said, The thing which thou hast spoken is good for us to do.
v.NASB: 'You answered me and said, 'The thing which you have said to do is good.'
v.NIV: You answered me, “What you propose to do is good.”
v.NRSV: You answered me, “The plan you have proposed is a good one.”
v.SAENEW: 그러자 당신들이 나에게 대답하기를 '말씀하신 대로 하는 것이 좋겠습니다' 하였습니다.
v.WLB: 너희가 내게 대답했다. ‘당신 생각이 좋습니다.’

v.CNV: 我就选出你们众支派中的领袖，就是有智慧有经验的人；按着你们的支派，立他们作管理你们的首领，就是作千夫长、百夫长、五十夫长、十夫长和其他官长。
v.ESV: So I took the heads of your tribes, wise and experienced men, and set them as heads over you, commanders of thousands, commanders of hundreds, commanders of fifties, commanders of tens, and officers, throughout your tribes.
v.GAE: 내가 너희 지파의 수령으로 지혜가 있고 인정 받는 자들을 취하여 너희의 수령을 삼되 곧 각 지파를 따라 천부장과 백부장과 오십부장과 십부장과 조장을 삼고
v.KJV: So I took the chief of your tribes, wise men, and known, and made them heads over you, captains over thousands, and captains over hundreds, and captains over fifties, and captains over tens, and officers among your tribes.
v.NASB: 'So I took the heads of your tribes, wise and experienced men, and appointed them heads over you, leaders of thousands and of hundreds, of fifties and of tens, and officers for your tribes.
v.NIV: So I took the leading men of your tribes, wise and respected men, and appointed them to have authority over you—as commanders of thousands, of hundreds, of fifties and of tens and as tribal officials.
v.NRSV: So I took the leaders of your tribes, wise and reputable individuals, and installed them as leaders over you, commanders of thousands, commanders of hundreds, commanders of fifties, commanders of tens, and officials, throughout your tribes.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 나는 당신들 가운데서 뽑은, 지혜가 있고 경험이 많은 사람을 당신들 각 지파의 대표로 세워서, 그들을 각 지파의 천부장과 백부장과 오십부장과 십부장으로 삼았고, 각 지파의 지도자로 삼았습니다.
v.WLB: 그리하여 내가 너희 지파에서 현명하고 존경할 만한 지도자들을 데려다가 너희를 다스릴 우두머리로 세웠다. 천부장들, 백부장들, 오십부장들, 십부장들과 지파의 지도자로 삼은 것이다.

v.CNV: “那时，我吩咐你们的审判官，说：‘你们在兄弟中听讼，无论是兄弟彼此诉讼，或是与同居的外地人诉讼，都要按着公义审判。
v.ESV: And I charged your judges at that time, ‘Hear the cases between your brothers, and judge righteously between a man and his brother or the alien who is with him.
v.GAE: 내가 그 때에 너희의 재판장들에게 명하여 이르기를 너희가 너희의 형제 중에서 송사를 들을 때에 쌍방간에 공정히 판결할 것이며 그들 중에 있는 타국인에게도 그리 할 것이라
v.KJV: And I charged your judges at that time, saying, Hear the causes between your brethren, and judge righteously between every man and his brother, and the stranger that is with him.
v.NASB: 'Then I charged your judges at that time, saying, 'Hear the cases between your fellow countrymen, and judge righteously between a man and his fellow countryman, or the alien who is with him.
v.NIV: And I charged your judges at that time, “Hear the disputes between your people and judge fairly, whether the case is between two Israelites or between an Israelite and a foreigner residing among you.
v.NRSV: I charged your judges at that time: “Give the members of your community a fair hearing, and judge rightly between one person and another, whether citizen or resident alien.
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 내가 당신들 재판관들에게 명령하였습니다. '당신들 동족 사이에 소송이 있거든, 잘 듣고 공정하게 재판하시오. 동족 사이에서만이 아니라, 동족과 외국인 사이의 소송에서도 그렇게 하시오.
v.WLB: 내가 그때 너희 재판장들에게 명령해 이스라엘 백성들 사이에 있는 사건이든, 이스라엘 사람과 이방 사람 사이의 사건이든 너희 형제들 사이의 분쟁을 잘 듣고 공정하게 심판하고

v.CNV: 审判的时候，不可偏袒人；无论贵贱尊卑，都要一样地听讼；不可怕人的情面，因为审判是属于　神的；如果你们遇见难断的案件，可以呈到我这里来，让我审断。’
v.ESV: You shall not be partial in judgment. You shall hear the small and the great alike. You shall not be intimidated by anyone, for the judgment is God's. And the case that is too hard for you, you shall bring to me, and I will hear it.’
v.GAE: 재판은 하나님께 속한 것인즉 너희는 재판할 때에 외모를 보지 말고 귀천을 차별 없이 듣고 사람의 낯을 두려워하지 말 것이며 스스로 결단하기 어려운 일이 있거든 내게로 돌리라 내가 들으리라 하였고
v.KJV: Ye shall not respect persons in judgment; but ye shall hear the small as well as the great; ye shall not be afraid of the face of man; for the judgment is God’s: and the cause that is too hard for you, bring it unto me, and I will hear it.
v.NASB: 'You shall not show partiality in judgment; you shall hear the small and the great alike. You shall not fear man, for the judgment is God's. The case that is too hard for you, you shall bring to me, and I will hear it.'
v.NIV: Do not show partiality in judging; hear both small and great alike. Do not be afraid of anyone, for judgment belongs to God. Bring me any case too hard for you, and I will hear it.”
v.NRSV: You must not be partial in judging: hear out the small and the great alike; you shall not be intimidated by anyone, for the judgment is God’s. Any case that is too hard for you, bring to me, and I will hear it.”
v.SAENEW: 재판은 하나님께 속한 것이니, 재판을 할 때에는 어느 한쪽 말만을 들으면 안 되오. 말할 기회는 세력이 있는 사람에게나 없는 사람에게나 똑같이 주어야 하오. 어떤 사람 앞에서도 두려워하지 마시오. 그리고 당신들이 판단하기 어려운 것이 있거든, 나에게로 가져 오시오. 내가 들어 보겠소.'
v.WLB: 편파적으로 하지 말고 낮은 사람이든 높은 사람이든 똑같이 대하며 너무 어려운 일이거든 내게 가져와 듣게 하라고 했다.

v.CNV: 那时，我把你们当作的一切事，都吩咐了你们。
v.ESV: And I commanded you at that time all the things that you should do.
v.GAE: 내가 너희의 행할 모든 일을 그 때에 너희에게 다 명령하였느니라
v.KJV: And I commanded you at that time all the things which ye should do.
v.NASB: 'I commanded you at that time all the things that you should do.
v.NIV: And at that time I told you everything you were to do.
v.NRSV: So I charged you at that time with all the things that you should do.
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 나는, 당신들이 해야 할 일을 모두 가르쳐 주었습니다."
v.WLB: 그때 내가 너희가 해야 할 모든 것을 말해 주었다.”

v.CNV: “我们照着耶和华我们的　神吩咐我们的，从何烈山起行，走过你们所见那大而可怕的旷野，沿着到亚摩利人山地的路而行；后来到了加低斯．巴尼亚。
v.ESV: “Then we set out from Horeb and went through all that great and terrifying wilderness that you saw, on the way to the hill country of the Amorites, as the Lord our God commanded us. And we came to Kadesh-barnea.
v.GAE: 우리 하나님 여호와께서 우리에게 명령하신 대로 우리가 호렙 산을 떠나 너희가 보았던 그 크고 두려운 광야를 지나 아모리 족속의 산지 길로 가데스 바네아에 이른 때에
v.KJV: And when we departed from Horeb, we went through all that great and terrible wilderness, which ye saw by the way of the mountain of the Amorites, as the LORD our God commanded us; and we came to Kadesh-barnea.
v.NASB: 'Then we set out from Horeb, and went through all that great and terrible wilderness which you saw on the way to the hill country of the Amorites, just as the LORD our God had commanded us; and we came to Kadesh-barnea.
v.NIV: Then, as the Lord our God commanded us, we set out from Horeb and went toward the hill country of the Amorites through all that vast and dreadful wilderness that you have seen, and so we reached Kadesh Barnea.
v.NRSV: Then, just as the LORD our God had ordered us, we set out from Horeb and went through all that great and terrible wilderness that you saw, on the way to the hill country of the Amorites, until we reached Kadesh-barnea.
v.SAENEW: "우리는 주 우리의 하나님이 우리에게 명하신 대로 하였습니다. 호렙을 떠나, 우리가 본 그 크고 무서운 광야를 지나서, 아모리 사람의 산지로 가는 길을 따라 가데스바네아까지 이르렀습니다.
v.WLB: “그때 우리 하나님 여호와께서 우리에게 명령하신 대로 우리가 호렙 산을 떠나 아모리 사람들의 산지로 향했는데 너희가 한 번도 본 적 없는 광활하고 무섭기 짝이 없는 그 광야를 거쳐 우리는 가데스 바네아에 도착했다.

v.CNV: 我对你们说：‘你们已经到了亚摩利人的山地，就是耶和华我们的　神赐给我们的。
v.ESV: And I said to you, ‘You have come to the hill country of the Amorites, which the Lord our God is giving us.
v.GAE: 내가 너희에게 이르기를 우리 하나님 여호와께서 우리에게 주신 아모리 족속의 산지에 너희가 이르렀나니
v.KJV: And I said unto you, Ye are come unto the mountain of the Amorites, which the LORD our God doth give unto us.
v.NASB: 'I said to you, 'You have come to the hill country of the Amorites which the LORD our God is about to give us.
v.NIV: Then I said to you, “You have reached the hill country of the Amorites, which the Lord our God is giving us.
v.NRSV: I said to you, “You have reached the hill country of the Amorites, which the LORD our God is giving us.
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 내가 당신들에게 일렀습니다. '이제 당신들은 주 우리의 하나님이 우리에게 주시는 아모리 사람의 산지까지 왔습니다.
v.WLB: 그때 내가 너희에게 말했다. ‘너희는 우리 하나님 여호와께서 우리에게 주시는 아모리 사람들의 산지에 다다랐다.

v.CNV: 看哪，耶和华你的　神已经把这地摆在你面前；你要照着耶和华你列祖的　神告诉你的上去，占领那地；不要害怕，也不要惊惶。’
v.ESV: See, the Lord your God has set the land before you. Go up, take possession, as the Lord , the God of your fathers, has told you. Do not fear or be dismayed.’
v.GAE: 너희의 하나님 여호와께서 이 땅을 너희 앞에 두셨은즉 너희 조상의 하나님 여호와께서 너희에게 이르신 대로 올라가서 차지하라 두려워하지 말라 주저하지 말라 한즉
v.KJV: Behold, the LORD thy God hath set the land before thee: go up and possess it, as the LORD God of thy fathers hath said unto thee; fear not, neither be discouraged.
v.NASB: 'See, the LORD your God has placed the land before you; go up, take possession, as the LORD, the God of your fathers, has spoken to you. Do not fear or be dismayed.'
v.NIV: See, the Lord your God has given you the land. Go up and take possession of it as the Lord , the God of your ancestors, told you. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged.”
v.NRSV: See, the LORD your God has given the land to you; go up, take possession, as the LORD, the God of your ancestors, has promised you; do not fear or be dismayed.”
v.SAENEW: 보십시오, 주 당신들의 하나님이 주신 땅이 당신들 앞에 있습니다. 주 당신들 조상의 하나님이 당신들에게 말씀하신 대로, 올라가서 차지하십시오. 두려워하지도 말고, 겁내지도 마십시오.'
v.WLB: 보라. 너희 하나님 여호와께서 이 땅을 너희에게 주셨다. 일어나 너희 조상들의 하나님 여호와께서 너희에게 말씀하신 대로 이 땅을 차지하라. 두려워 말라. 망설이지 말라.’

v.CNV: 你们众人都走到我这里来，说：‘我们要先派些人去，替我们窥探那地，然后把我们上去该走哪一条路，该进哪些城，向我们回报。’
v.ESV: Then all of you came near me and said, ‘Let us send men before us, that they may explore the land for us and bring us word again of the way by which we must go up and the cities into which we shall come.’
v.GAE: 너희가 다 내 앞으로 나아와 말하기를 우리가 사람을 우리보다 먼저 보내어 우리를 위하여 그 땅을 정탐하고 어느 길로 올라가야 할 것과 어느 성읍으로 들어가야 할 것을 우리에게 알리게 하자 하기에
v.KJV: And ye came near unto me every one of you, and said, We will send men before us, and they shall search us out the land, and bring us word again by what way we must go up, and into what cities we shall come.
v.NASB: 'Then all of you approached me and said, 'Let us send men before us, that they may search out the land for us, and bring back to us word of the way by which we should go up and the cities which we shall enter.'
v.NIV: Then all of you came to me and said, “Let us send men ahead to spy out the land for us and bring back a report about the route we are to take and the towns we will come to.”
v.NRSV: All of you came to me and said, “Let us send men ahead of us to explore the land for us and bring back a report to us regarding the route by which we should go up and the cities we will come to.”
v.SAENEW: 그러나 당신들은 다 나에게 와서 이렇게 말하였습니다. '땅을 탐지할 사람들을 먼저 보내서, 우리가 올라갈 길과 우리가 쳐들어갈 성읍들이 어떠한지, 그 땅을 정찰하여 우리에게 보고하게 하자.'
v.WLB: 그러자 너희 모두는 내게 와서 말했다. ‘정탐꾼을 아모리 땅에 먼저 보내 우리가 어떤 길을 택할지, 우리가 갈 그 성들이 어떤지 보고 돌아오게 합시다.’

v.CNV: 这话我很同意，就从你们中间选了十二个人，每支派一人。
v.ESV: The thing seemed good to me, and I took twelve men from you, one man from each tribe.
v.GAE: 내가 그 말을 좋게 여겨 너희 중 각 지파에서 한 사람씩 열둘을 택하매
v.KJV: and the saying pleased me well: and I took twelve men of you, one of a tribe:
v.NASB: 'The thing pleased me and I took twelve of your men, one man for each tribe.
v.NIV: The idea seemed good to me; so I selected twelve of you, one man from each tribe.
v.NRSV: The plan seemed good to me, and I selected twelve of you, one from each tribe.
v.SAENEW: 내가 듣기에도 그 말은 옳은 말이어서, 나는 각 지파에서 한 사람씩 열두 사람을 뽑았습니다.
v.WLB: 그 생각은 좋아 보였다. 그래서 내가 너희 가운데 각 지파에서 한 명씩 12명을 뽑았다.

v.CNV: 于是他们转身出发，上了山地，到了以实各谷，窥探那地。
v.ESV: And they turned and went up into the hill country, and came to the Valley of Eshcol and spied it out.
v.GAE: 그들이 돌이켜 산지에 올라 에스골 골짜기에 이르러 그 곳을 정탐하고
v.KJV: And they turned and went up into the mountain, and came unto the valley of Eshcol, and searched it out.
v.NASB: 'They turned and went up into the hill country, and came to the valley of Eshcol and spied it out.
v.NIV: They left and went up into the hill country, and came to the Valley of Eshkol and explored it.
v.NRSV: They set out and went up into the hill country, and when they reached the Valley of Eshcol they spied it out
v.SAENEW: 뽑힌 사람들은 산지로 올라가서, 에스골 골짜기에 이르기까지, 그 땅을 두루 다니면서 탐지하였습니다.
v.WLB: 그들은 길을 떠나 그 산지로 올라가서 에스골 골짜기로 들어가 그곳을 정탐했다.

v.CNV: 他们手里拿着那地的果子，带下来到我们这里，并且向我们回报，说：‘耶和华我们的　神要赐给我们的地是美地。’
v.ESV: And they took in their hands some of the fruit of the land and brought it down to us, and brought us word again and said, ‘It is a good land that the Lord our God is giving us.’
v.GAE: 그 땅의 열매를 손에 가지고 우리에게로 돌아와서 우리에게 말하여 이르되 우리의 하나님 여호와께서 우리에게 주시는 땅이 좋더라 하였느니라
v.KJV: And they took of the fruit of the land in their hands, and brought it down unto us, and brought us word again, and said, It is a good land which the LORD our God doth give us.
v.NASB: 'Then they took some of the fruit of the land in their hands and brought it down to us; and they brought us back a report and said, 'It is a good land which the LORD our God is about to give us.'
v.NIV: Taking with them some of the fruit of the land, they brought it down to us and reported, “It is a good land that the Lord our God is giving us.”
v.NRSV: and gathered some of the land’s produce, which they brought down to us. They brought back a report to us, and said, “It is a good land that the LORD our God is giving us.”
v.SAENEW: 그들은 그 땅에서 난 열매들을 따 가지고 내려와서 우리에게 보고하기를, '주 우리의 하나님이 우리에게 주신 땅이 좋다'고 하였습니다.
v.WLB: 그들은 그 땅에서 난 과일들을 가지고 우리에게 내려와서 보고했다. ‘우리 하나님 여호와께서 우리에게 주시는 땅은 좋은 땅입니다.’”

v.CNV: “但你们不肯上去，竟违背了耶和华你们的　神的吩咐；
v.ESV: “Yet you would not go up, but rebelled against the command of the Lord your God.
v.GAE: 그러나 너희가 올라가기를 원하지 아니하고 너희의 하나님 여호와의 명령을 거역하여
v.KJV: Notwithstanding ye would not go up, but rebelled against the commandment of the LORD your God:
v.NASB: 'Yet you were not willing to go up, but rebelled against the command of the LORD your God;
v.NIV: But you were unwilling to go up; you rebelled against the command of the Lord your God.
v.NRSV: But you were unwilling to go up. You rebelled against the command of the LORD your God;
v.SAENEW: 그러나 당신들은 주 당신들의 하나님의 말씀을 거역하고, 올라가려고 하지 않았습니다.
v.WLB: “그러나 너희는 올라가고 싶어 하지 않았다. 너희는 너희 하나님 여호와의 명령을 거역한 것이다.

v.CNV: 你们在帐幕里抱怨，说：‘耶和华因为恨我们，所以把我们从埃及地领出来，要把我们交在亚摩利人手中，消灭我们。
v.ESV: And you murmured in your tents and said, ‘Because the Lord hated us he has brought us out of the land of Egypt, to give us into the hand of the Amorites, to destroy us.
v.GAE: 장막 중에서 원망하여 이르기를 여호와께서 우리를 미워하시므로 아모리 족속의 손에 넘겨 멸하시려고 우리를 애굽 땅에서 인도하여 내셨도다
v.KJV: And ye murmured in your tents, and said, Because the LORD hated us, he hath brought us forth out of the land of Egypt, to deliver us into the hand of the Amorites, to destroy us.

v.WLB: 너희는 너희 장막 안에서 원망하며 말했다. ‘여호와께서 우리를 미워하셔서 우리를 이집트에서 이끌어 내 아모리 사람들의 손에 넘겨 우리를 멸망시키시려는 것 아닌가?

v.CNV: 我们上哪里去呢？我们的兄弟使我们心灰意冷，说：那地的人比我们高大；他们的城邑又大又坚固，高耸入天；我们在那里还看见有亚衲族的人。’

v.CNV: 我就对你们说：‘不要惊恐，不要害怕他们。

v.KJV: Then I said unto you, Dread not, neither be afraid of them.
v.NASB: 'Then I said to you, 'Do not be shocked, nor fear them.

v.GAE: 너희보다 먼저 가시는 너희의 하나님 여호와께서 애굽에서 너희를 위하여 너희 목전에서 모든 일을 행하신 것 같이 이제도 너희를 위하여 싸우실 것이며
v.KJV: The LORD your God which goeth before you, he shall fight for you, according to all that he did for you in Egypt before your eyes;
v.NASB: 'The LORD your God who goes before you will Himself fight on your behalf, just as He did for you in Egypt before your eyes,
v.NIV: The Lord your God, who is going before you, will fight for you, as he did for you in Egypt, before your very eyes,
v.NRSV: The LORD your God, who goes before you, is the one who will fight for you, just as he did for you in Egypt before your very eyes,
v.SAENEW: 당신들 앞에서 당신들을 인도하여 주시는 주 당신들의 하나님은, 이집트에서 당신들이 보는 앞에서 당신들을 대신하여 모든 일을 하신 것과 같이, 이제도 당신들을 대신하여 싸우실 것이오.
v.WLB: 너희보다 앞서 가시는 너희 하나님 여호와께서 이집트에서 너희를 위해 하셨듯이 너희 눈앞에서 너희를 위해 싸우실 것이다.

v.CNV: 并且在旷野里，你们也看见了耶和华你们的　神，怎样在你们所行的一切路上，背负你们，好像人背负自己的儿子一样，直到你们来到了这地方。’
v.ESV: and in the wilderness, where you have seen how the Lord your God carried you, as a man carries his son, all the way that you went until you came to this place.’
v.GAE: 광야에서도 너희가 당하였거니와 사람이 자기의 아들을 안는 것 같이 너희의 하나님 여호와께서 너희가 걸어온 길에서 너희를 안으사 이 곳까지 이르게 하셨느니라 하나
v.KJV: And in the wilderness, where thou hast seen how that the LORD thy God bare thee, as a man doth bear his son, in all the way that ye went, until ye came into this place.

v.NIV: and in the wilderness. There you saw how the Lord your God carried you, as a father carries his son, all the way you went until you reached this place.”

v.WLB: 광야에서도 너희가 보지 않았느냐? 너희 하나님 여호와께서 아버지가 아들을 안는 것같이 너희를 안아 너희가 이곳에 닿을 때까지 그 여정을 함께하시지 않았느냐?’

v.KJV: Who went in the way before you, to search you out a place to pitch your tents in, in fire by night, to shew you by what way ye should go, and in a cloud by day.
v.NASB: who goes before you on your way, to seek out a place for you to encamp, in fire by night and cloud by day, to show you the way in which you should go.
v.NIV: who went ahead of you on your journey, in fire by night and in a cloud by day, to search out places for you to camp and to show you the way you should go.

v.SAENEW: 당신들이 진 칠 곳을 찾아 주시려고 당신들 앞에서 당신들을 인도하여 주셨는데도, 그리고 당신들이 갈 길을 보여 주시려고 밤에는 불기둥으로 낮에는 구름기둥으로 인도하여 주셨는데도, 당신들은 아직도 주 당신들의 하나님을 믿지 않습니다."

v.KJV: Surely there shall not one of these men of this evil generation see that good land, which I sware to give unto your fathers,

v.NIV: “No one from this evil generation shall see the good land I swore to give your ancestors,

v.KJV: Save Caleb the son of Jephunneh; he shall see it, and to him will I give the land that he hath trodden upon, and to his children, because he hath wholly followed the LORD.

v.NIV: except Caleb son of Jephunneh. He will see it, and I will give him and his descendants the land he set his feet on, because he followed the Lord wholeheartedly. ”

v.CNV: 至于你们，你们要转回，经往红海的路，起程到旷野里去。’

v.NIV: But as for you, turn around and set out toward the desert along the route to the Red Sea. ”

v.SAENEW: 너희는 발길을 돌려서, 홍해로 가는 길을 따라 광야로 가거라' 하셨습니다.
v.WLB: 그러나 너희는 뒤돌아 홍해 길을 따라 난 광야를 향해 가라.’

v.CNV: “那时你们回答我，说：‘我们得罪了耶和华；现在我们愿意照着耶和华我们　神的一切吩咐，上去作战。’于是你们都预备了兵器，轻率地上山去了。
v.ESV: “Then you answered me, ‘We have sinned against the Lord . We ourselves will go up and fight, just as the Lord our God commanded us.’ And every one of you fastened on his weapons of war and thought it easy to go up into the hill country.
v.GAE: 너희가 대답하여 내게 이르기를 우리가 여호와께 범죄하였사오니 우리 하나님께서 우리에게 명령하신 대로 우리가 올라가서 싸우리이다 하고 너희가 각각 무기를 가지고 경솔히 산지로 올라가려 할 때에
v.KJV: Then ye answered and said unto me, We have sinned against the LORD, we will go up and fight, according to all that the LORD our God commanded us. And when ye had girded on every man his weapons of war, ye were ready to go up into the hill.
v.NASB: 'Then you said to me, 'We have sinned against the LORD; we will indeed go up and fight, just as the LORD our God commanded us.' And every man of you girded on his weapons of war, and regarded it as easy to go up into the hill country.
v.NIV: Then you replied, “We have sinned against the Lord . We will go up and fight, as the Lord our God commanded us.” So every one of you put on his weapons, thinking it easy to go up into the hill country.
v.NRSV: You answered me, “We have sinned against the LORD! We are ready to go up and fight, just as the LORD our God commanded us.” So all of you strapped on your battle gear, and thought it easy to go up into the hill country.
v.SAENEW: 그러자 당신들이 나에게 말하기를 '우리가 우리 주님께 죄를 지었으니, 주 우리의 하나님이 우리에게 명하신 대로 다 올라가 싸우자' 하였습니다. 그리고는 각자 자기의 무기를 들고, 경솔하게 그 산지로 올라갔습니다.
v.WLB: 그러자 너희가 대답했다. ‘우리가 여호와께 죄를 지었으니 우리 하나님 여호와께서 우리에게 명령하신 대로 우리가 올라가 싸우겠습니다.’ 그리하여 너희는 그 산지에 올라가는 것이 쉬울 것이라 생각하고 자기의 무기를 들었다.

v.CNV: 耶和华对我说：‘你要对他们说：“你们不要上去，也不要作战，因为我不在你们中间，免得你们在仇敌面前被击败。”’
v.ESV: And the Lord said to me, ‘Say to them, Do not go up or fight, for I am not in your midst, lest you be defeated before your enemies.’
v.GAE: 여호와께서 내게 이르시되 너는 그들에게 이르기를 너희는 올라가지 말라 싸우지도 말라 내가 너희 중에 있지 아니하니 너희가 대적에게 패할까 하노라 하시기로
v.KJV: And the LORD said unto me, Say unto them, Go not up, neither fight; for I am not among you; lest ye be smitten before your enemies.
v.NASB: 'And the LORD said to me, 'Say to them, 'Do not go up nor fight, for I am not among you; otherwise you will be defeated before your enemies.''
v.NIV: But the Lord said to me, “Tell them, ‘Do not go up and fight, because I will not be with you. You will be defeated by your enemies.’”
v.NRSV: The LORD said to me, “Say to them, ‘Do not go up and do not fight, for I am not in the midst of you; otherwise you will be defeated by your enemies.’”
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 주님께서 나에게 말씀하시기를 '그들에게 전하여라. 너희는 올라가지도 말고 싸우지도 말아라. 내가 너희 가운데 있지 않으니, 너희가 적에게 패할 것이다' 하셨습니다.
v.WLB: 그러나 여호와께서 내게 말씀하셨다. ‘그들에게 말하여라. ‘그리로 올라가 싸우지 말라. 내가 너희와 함께하지 않을 것이다. 너희는 너희 적들에게 지고 말 것이다.’

v.CNV: 我就告诉你们，你们却不听从，竟违背耶和华的吩咐，擅自上山去了。
v.ESV: So I spoke to you, and you would not listen; but you rebelled against the command of the Lord and presumptuously went up into the hill country.
v.GAE: 내가 너희에게 말하였으나 너희가 듣지 아니하고 여호와의 명령을 거역하고 거리낌 없이 산지로 올라가매
v.KJV: So I spake unto you; and ye would not hear, but rebelled against the commandment of the LORD, and went presumptuously up into the hill.
v.NASB: 'So I spoke to you, but you would not listen. Instead you rebelled against the command of the LORD, and acted presumptuously and went up into the hill country.
v.NIV: So I told you, but you would not listen. You rebelled against the Lord ’s command and in your arrogance you marched up into the hill country.
v.NRSV: Although I told you, you would not listen. You rebelled against the command of the LORD and presumptuously went up into the hill country.
v.SAENEW: 내가 이 말씀을 당신들에게 전하였지만 당신들은 듣지 않았고, 주님의 말씀을 거역하고서, 당신들 마음대로 산지로 올라갔습니다.
v.WLB: 그리하여 내가 너희에게 이 말을 전했지만 너희는 듣지 않고 여호와의 명령에 반역해 교만하게도 그 산지로 행진해 올라갔던 것이다.

v.CNV: 住在那山上的亚摩利人，就出来攻击你们，好像蜂群一样追赶你们，在西珥击杀你们，直到何珥玛。
v.ESV: Then the Amorites who lived in that hill country came out against you and chased you as bees do and beat you down in Seir as far as Hormah.
v.GAE: 그 산지에 거주하는 아모리 족속이 너희에게 마주 나와 벌 떼 같이 너희를 쫓아 세일 산에서 쳐서 호르마까지 이른지라
v.KJV: And the Amorites, which dwelt in that mountain, came out against you, and chased you, as bees do, and destroyed you in Seir, even unto Hormah.
v.NASB: 'The Amorites who lived in that hill country came out against you and chased you as bees do, and crushed you from Seir to Hormah.
v.NIV: The Amorites who lived in those hills came out against you; they chased you like a swarm of bees and beat you down from Seir all the way to Hormah.
v.NRSV: The Amorites who lived in that hill country then came out against you and chased you as bees do. They beat you down in Seir as far as Hormah.
v.SAENEW: 그러자 그 산지에 살던 아모리 사람이 당신들을 보고, 벌떼 같이 쫓아나와서, 세일에서 호르마까지 뒤쫓으면서 당신들을 쳤습니다.
v.WLB: 그 산지에 살고 있던 아모리 사람들이 나와 너희를 쳤다. 그들은 너희를 벌 떼처럼 쫓아냈고 세일에서 호르마까지 너희를 모조리 쳐서 쓰러뜨렸다.

v.CNV: 你们就回来，在耶和华面前哭，但耶和华不听你们的声音，也不向你们侧耳。
v.ESV: And you returned and wept before the Lord , but the Lord did not listen to your voice or give ear to you.
v.GAE: 너희가 돌아와 여호와 앞에서 통곡하나 여호와께서 너희의 소리를 듣지 아니하시며 너희에게 귀를 기울이지 아니하셨으므로
v.KJV: And ye returned and wept before the LORD; but the LORD would not hearken to your voice, nor give ear unto you.
v.NASB: 'Then you returned and wept before the LORD; but the LORD did not listen to your voice nor give ear to you.
v.NIV: You came back and wept before the Lord , but he paid no attention to your weeping and turned a deaf ear to you.
v.NRSV: When you returned and wept before the LORD, the LORD would neither heed your voice nor pay you any attention.
v.SAENEW: 당신들이 돌아와 주님 앞에서 통곡을 했지만, 주님께서는 당신들의 소리를 듣지 않으시고, 귀도 기울이지 않으셨습니다."
v.WLB: 너희는 돌아와 여호와 앞에서 울었지만 여호와께서는 너희가 우는 것을 돌보지 않으셨고 너희에게서 귀를 막으셨다.

v.CNV: 于是你们在加低斯住了许多日子。”
v.ESV: So you remained at Kadesh many days, the days that you remained there.
v.GAE: 너희가 가데스에 여러 날 동안 머물렀나니 곧 너희가 그 곳에 머물던 날 수대로니라
v.KJV: So ye abode in Kadesh many days, according unto the days that ye abode there.
v.NASB: 'So you remained in Kadesh many days, the days that you spent there.
v.NIV: And so you stayed in Kadesh many days—all the time you spent there.
v.NRSV: After you had stayed at Kadesh as many days as you did,
v.SAENEW: "우리가 가데스에 머무르며 그렇게 많은 날을 보내고 난 뒤에,
v.WLB: 그리하여 너희가 여러 날을 가데스에서 머물렀고 너희는 그곳에서 오랜 시간을 보냈던 것이다.”
